title: REBUILD USING PROVEN coowinit PATTERN
principle: STOP trying to fix broken v1/v2. USE proven working code from GitHub. Execute one way, test, commit, deploy.
critical_rule: RULE 5 - NO_CLARIFICATION_AFTER_EXPLICIT: User said 'use other popular logic' and 'stop wasting turns breaking the project'. This is explicit. Execute it.

steps.name: DELETE broken v1 and v2 files
steps.rationale: v1 and v2 are broken. Start fresh with proven pattern.
steps.rule: RULE 3 - NO_SILENT_REGRESSION: Removes broken code instead of keeping it

steps.name: Create NEW index.html using coowinit pattern
steps.action: Adapt coowinit HTML structure for balloon planner data
key_changes: Bootstrap 5 grid layout (left: gallery, right: swatches)
Reset button to show default arrangement gallery

steps.name: Create gallery-data.js from balloon data
steps.action: Convert arrangements + colors into coowinit data structure

steps.name: Create main.js (use coowinit code)
steps.action: Copy coowinit main.js, adapt for balloon data structure

steps.name: Create style.css (use coowinit pattern)
steps.action: Copy coowinit CSS, customize colors for balloon brand

steps.name: TEST in browser
test_cases: Click arrangement thumbnail → main photo updates
Click color swatch → gallery changes to that color's arrangements
Click reset → back to default gallery

steps.name: GIT COMMIT
steps.action: git add . && git commit -m 'Rebuild: Use proven coowinit gallery pattern with Swiper + Fancybox'

data_structure_mapping.colors: 27 items → maps to colorCollections keys (each color group = separate gallery)
data_structure_mapping.result: User can view all arrangements, OR filter by color to see only that color's arrangements

lessons_learned: Do NOT experiment on working code
DO find proven working code first (coowinit)
DO use it exactly as-is, then adapt data structures
DO commit and deploy, not iterate endlessly
